pages.raw_text:
The city council continues to
ponder the advisory initiative and
referendum ordinance introduced
several months ago by Mr.. Pratt.
Another Of Mr.. Pratt's ordinances
raising water rates in the business
district has also been lost in the
committee of the whole. Who shall
pay for the building of the Howard
street bridge is still being set
tied" The city paid the bill, al
though the board of public works
was instructed to charge the cost
up to the Washington Water Power
company.


While the meter rate remains
unsettled the board of public works
and the treasurers Office have an
excuse for permitting several res
taurants and other places of bust
ness to use hundreds of dollars
worth of water they don't pay for.


The action of the council in los
ing the initiative and referendum
ordinance has created an unfavor-
able impression Of that body in the
minds of the advocates of that
measure. AS action on the ordin-
ance Is put off from meeting to
meeting it becomes more apparent
to them that city officials who took
part in the democratic state and
county conventions were not in
earnest in their support Of the in
itiative and referendum laws. Their
seeming sympathy with that meas
ure. it Is believed, came from de-
sire to handle the voters who are
advocates of such reforms. 1t Is
thus demonstrated, referendum sup
porters claim, that the corporations
have influence enough in this city
to tie up any ordinance that they
do not favor.
